mnemonics: LINK - Lists In Nodes, Know locations
keyPoints: Static allocation uses a single contiguous block of memory allocated at compile time.
Linked allocation uses non-contiguous memory blocks (nodes) connected by pointers, allocated at runtime.
Static allocation has fixed size, while linked allocation can grow and shrink dynamically.
commonMistakes: Assuming linked allocation requires less total memory (pointers add overhead).
Thinking static allocation is always faster (it depends on the operation being performed).
Believing you can insert elements anywhere in a static array without shifting elements.
